item.title: Arfin India Joins Reliable Performers List Following Strong Q4 Financials
item.description: Arfin India, a micro-cap player in the non-ferrous metals sector, has been added to the esteemed Reliable Performers list as of 11 May 2026, following a series of outstanding quarterly results that underscore its improving fundamentals and growth trajectory. The stock’s recent bullish momentum and significant financial improvements position it as a compelling addition for investors seeking consistent performance in a challenging market environment. Time: 21 minutes ago

item.title: Shyam Metalics Q4 FY26: Strong Quarter Masks Valuation Concerns
item.description: Shyam Metalics and Energy Ltd., one of India's largest ferro alloy producers and the fourth-largest player in the sponge iron industry, delivered a robust quarter-on-quarter performance in Q4 FY26, with consolidated net profit surging 61.78% to ₹319.09 crores from ₹197.24 crores in Q3 FY26. On a year-on-year basis, the company posted a healthy 45.87% growth versus Q4 FY25's ₹218.75 crores. However, the stock faced selling pressure, declining 3.55% to ₹871.60 on May 11, 2026, as investors grappled with stretched valuations and mixed technical signals despite the strong operational performance. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: NDR Auto Components Q4 FY26: Strong Quarter Masks Valuation Concerns
item.description: NDR Auto Components Ltd., a small-cap player in India's automotive components industry, delivered a robust Q4 FY26 performance with consolidated net profit surging 21.46% quarter-on-quarter to ₹18.45 crores, marking the company's strongest quarterly earnings to date. Despite this operational momentum, the stock trades at a demanding valuation of 33 times trailing earnings, prompting analysts to maintain a cautious stance on the ₹1,950-crore market capitalisation company. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Expo Engineering Q4 FY26: Sharp Decline Raises Serious Concerns
item.description: Expo Engineering and Projects Ltd., a micro-cap player in the industrial products sector with a market capitalisation of ₹151.00 crores, reported deeply concerning results for Q4 FY26, posting a net loss of ₹0.66 crores compared to a profit of ₹1.57 crores in Q4 FY25—a complete reversal from profitability to loss. The stock declined 2.99% following the announcement, closing at ₹65.78 on May 11, 2026, reflecting investor unease over the company's deteriorating financial trajectory. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: UPL Q4 FY26: Strong Quarter Masks Underlying Concerns
item.description: UPL Ltd., India's largest pesticides and agrochemicals manufacturer with a market capitalisation of ₹54,900 crores, delivered a robust Q4 FY26 performance with consolidated net profit surging 167.93% quarter-on-quarter to ₹1,061.00 crores. However, the year-on-year comparison reveals a more modest 18.42% growth, whilst the stock remains under pressure, trading 15.84% below its year-to-date opening levels despite a recent 3.51% single-day bounce to ₹668.80. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: SRG Housing Finance Q4 FY26: Strong Quarter Marred by Expensive Valuation and Weak Institutional Interest
item.description: SRG Housing Finance Ltd., a Rajasthan-focused housing finance company, reported a robust fourth quarter for FY26, with net profit surging 49.43% year-on-year to ₹9.25 crores, marking the highest quarterly profit in the company's recent history. However, despite the impressive operational performance, the stock has delivered underwhelming returns of just 3.41% over the past year, significantly lagging the housing finance sector's 48.16% gain, whilst trading at an expensive valuation with minimal institutional backing. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: PVR Inox Q4 FY26: Strong Recovery Marred by Inconsistent Quarterly Performance
item.description: PVR Inox Ltd., India's largest multiplex operator with 1,754 screens across 361 properties, posted a consolidated net profit of ₹186.70 crores in Q4 FY26 (March quarter), marking a dramatic 95.09% quarter-on-quarter surge but plunging 249.36% year-on-year from a loss of ₹125.00 crores in the same period last year. The stock faced significant pressure following the results announcement, declining 4.46% to ₹1,026.15 on May 11, 2026, wiping out gains from earlier in the session when it touched an intraday high of ₹1,108.05. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Are Paisalo Digital Ltd latest results good or bad?
item.description: Paisalo Digital Ltd's latest results show strong revenue growth and record net profit, but face challenges with margin compression and a decline in promoter holding, indicating mixed performance overall. Investors should monitor these factors closely. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Oasis Securities Q4 FY26: Losses Mount as NBFC Struggles with Erratic Performance
item.description: Oasis Securities Ltd., a micro-cap non-banking financial company, reported a net loss of ₹0.58 crores in Q4 FY26 (March 2026 quarter), marking a sharp deterioration from the ₹0.42 crore profit posted in the preceding quarter. The loss represents a stark reversal in the company's erratic quarterly performance, with revenues plunging 66.07% sequentially to just ₹0.19 crores. The stock, trading at ₹15.20 with a market capitalisation of ₹30.00 crores, has declined 48.39% over the past year, significantly underperforming both its NBFC sector peers and the broader market. Time: 4 hours ago

item.title: Golden Cross Forms in PMC Fincorp Ltd — On a Day the Stock Fell 2.45%. What the Mixed Signals Mean
item.description: The 50-day moving average has crossed above the 200-day moving average for PMC Fincorp Ltd, signalling a golden cross on 11 May 2026. Yet, the stock declined 2.45% on the same day, while monthly momentum indicators remain bearish. This juxtaposition of signals calls for a detailed examination of the technical and fundamental context behind the crossover. Time: 4 hours ago

item.title: Iris Clothings Q4 FY26: Strong Quarter Masks Valuation Concerns
item.description: Iris Clothings Ltd., a niche player in the branded kids' wear segment, delivered a robust performance in Q4 FY26 (January-March 2026), posting net profit of ₹6.43 crores—a remarkable 43.52% quarter-on-quarter surge and 43.53% year-on-year growth. The Howrah-based company, with a market capitalisation of ₹713.00 crores, demonstrated strong operational momentum as revenues surged to ₹60.48 crores, marking the highest quarterly sales in its recent history. However, trading at a price-to-earnings ratio of 43 times—significantly above the industry average of 26 times—the stock's valuation remains a critical concern for investors despite the impressive operational performance. Time: 4 hours ago

item.title: Golden Cross Forms in Flair Writing Industries Ltd — On a Day the Stock Fell 0.73%. What the Mixed Signals Mean
item.description: The 50-day moving average has crossed above the 200-day moving average for Flair Writing Industries Ltd, signalling a golden cross on 11 May 2026. Yet, the stock declined 0.73% on the same day, and monthly technical indicators remain inconclusive, presenting a complex picture for investors analysing this signal. Time: 4 hours ago

item.title: Suryoday Small Finance Bank Ltd Forms Golden Cross Amid Mixed Technical Signals
item.description: The 50-day moving average has crossed above the 200-day moving average for Suryoday Small Finance Bank Ltd, signalling a golden cross on 11 May 2026. Yet, the stock slipped 0.58% on the day the cross formed, while monthly RSI remains bearish. This juxtaposition of signals calls for a detailed examination of the technical and fundamental backdrop to assess the signal’s reliability. Time: 4 hours ago
